note: Fictional dataset for the AEO/GEO audit showcase. All Northwind Pay scores and evidence are invented; cited URLs are illustrative and do not resolve.
summary: Cleanly vettable by a model — and the reason the assessment track is strong. Content is fully server-rendered, the raw HTML carries an accurate Organization / FinancialService schema plus FAQPage and Person markup, and a complete llms.txt maps the site. The brand is firmly bound to its name (no sample-database confusion in answers). The main miss is gated pricing; everything a model needs to describe Northwind correctly by name is present and fetchable.

schema_markup.rationale: Well-built JSON-LD in the raw HTML, not boilerplate. The homepage carries an Organization block with correct legalName ('Northwind Pay, Inc.'), founding date, NYC address, and a hasCredential entry for the NYDFS BitLicense and MSB registration. Product pages (/pay-ins, /payouts, /conversions) add a FinancialService entity with serviceType arrays, areaServed (40+ countries), and a BusinessAudience of 'PSPs, fintechs, and platforms.' The /company page has Person schema for both founders, and the FAQ page carries FAQPage / Question / acceptedAnswer markup. This is comprehensive enough that a model can pin the current facts — which is why by-name answers stay accurate and current.
evidence: Homepage: Organization legalName 'Northwind Pay, Inc.', foundingDate '2021', address 'New York, NY', hasCredential 'NYDFS BitLicense' + 'FinCEN MSB'
/payouts: FinancialService 'Stablecoin Payouts', serviceType [Stablecoin Payouts, Off-ramp, Conversion], areaServed '40+ countries', audience BusinessAudience 'PSPs, fintechs, payout platforms'
/company: Person schema for Dana Whitfield (CEO, co-founder) and Marcus Lindqvist (CTO, co-founder)
/faq: FAQPage with Question + acceptedAnswer for 'What is Northwind Pay?', 'Is Northwind Pay regulated?', 'What stablecoins does it support?'

claim_sentence_density.rationale: Pages lead with declarative, liftable claim sentences rather than vague marketing copy. The homepage and product pages state what Northwind is, who it serves, and how it is regulated in clean sentences a model can quote verbatim — which is exactly what shows up, near word-for-word, in the assessment answers.
evidence: Homepage: 'Northwind Pay is regulated stablecoin payment infrastructure: one API for pay-ins, payouts, and conversions.'
Homepage: 'Move dollars onchain without building custody, licensing, or node operations in-house.'
/compliance: 'Northwind Pay is a registered FinCEN MSB with U.S. state money transmitter licenses and a NYDFS BitLicense; customer balances are segregated at a qualified custodian.'
/payouts: 'Send single and mass stablecoin payouts with off-ramp to local bank rails in 40+ countries.'

fetchability_no_js.rationale: No JS-gating. A no-JS fetch of the homepage returns the full headings, product names, the one-liner, the regulatory summary, and the founder names in raw HTML — a crawler sees essentially everything a browser sees. This is the foundation of the clean assessment performance: the model never has to guess.
evidence: Raw curl homepage: 6 <h1>/<h2> with product and positioning copy, ~4,800 visible text chars, no content loaded only via JS
/company raw HTML already contains '2021', 'New York', founder names, and 'Series B'
No content found only in the rendered version and missing from raw HTML

pricing_clarity.rationale: Pricing is fully gated. Every conversion routes through 'Talk to sales' / 'Get API keys' with no public price list, tiers, or per-unit rates on the page. Expected for a B2B infrastructure seller, but for the vetting job it means a model asked 'how much does Northwind Pay cost' can only answer 'contact sales.' Marginally above floor because directional proof points exist in marketing copy (per-transaction payout fee, FX spread on conversions, no minimum on the developer tier), but they are not structured on the core pages.
evidence: Homepage: 14x 'Talk to sales' / 'Get API keys' CTA, 0 pricing tables
/payouts and /conversions: no published rates
No /pricing page; directional fee language only in blog/marketing copy

team_about_clarity.rationale: Strong, crawler-visible about content. The /company page states the mission verbatim, names both founders (Dana Whitfield, CEO; Marcus Lindqvist, CTO) with Person schema, the founding year (2021), the NYC HQ, the Series B stage, and a short funding summary. 'What we do' is unambiguous across the home and company pages, and the founders are linked out to authoritative profiles.
evidence: /company: 'Our mission is to make moving dollars onchain as boring and reliable as a card transaction.'
/company: 'Founded 2021 in New York City', 'Dana Whitfield, CEO & co-founder', 'Marcus Lindqvist, CTO & co-founder', 'Series B'
/company: founder cards link to LinkedIn profiles

faq_presence.rationale: A substantive FAQ page exists, its Q&A is server-rendered, and it carries proper FAQPage / Question / acceptedAnswer JSON-LD — so a model can both read and structurally parse it. The questions skew correctly toward brand-vetting ('What is Northwind Pay?', 'Is Northwind Pay regulated?', 'Which stablecoins and countries are supported?'). Held from 5 only because the answer copy is brief and a few high-intent questions ('How does pricing work?', 'Who are typical customers?') are missing.
evidence: /faq: FAQPage JSON-LD present with acceptedAnswer for 'What is Northwind Pay?', 'Is Northwind Pay regulated?', 'What stablecoins do you support?', 'Which countries can I pay out to?'
Answers are server-rendered and crawlable
Gaps: no pricing or customer-reference questions in the FAQ set

internal_linking.rationale: Coherent, crawlable internal structure. The homepage links to all key destinations (/pay-ins, /payouts, /conversions, /compliance, /company, /blog, /docs) via clean absolute paths, robots.txt allows crawling and declares the sitemap, and the developer docs are cross-linked from product pages. Minor: the blog and docs live on a subdomain (docs.northwindpay.com) and the insights/research surface that would anchor the category terms does not yet exist.
evidence: Homepage links: /pay-ins, /payouts, /conversions, /compliance, /company, /blog, /docs
robots.txt: 'User-agent: * / Allow: /' + 'Sitemap: https://www.northwindpay.com/sitemap.xml'
docs cross-linked from each product page

llms_txt.rationale: Present, complete, and accurate (light signal, not weighted heavily). https://www.northwindpay.com/llms.txt returns HTTP 200, text/plain, with a one-line description and section-organized annotated links (Company, Products, Compliance, Developers), and the facts are correct (regulated MSB, single API, pay-ins/payouts/conversions). Above the norm for the category.
evidence: GET /llms.txt -> HTTP 200, content-type text/plain
Header: '# Northwind Pay > Regulated stablecoin payment infrastructure: one API for pay-ins, payouts, and conversions.'
Sections present: Company, Products, Compliance, Developers — each with annotated links

fix_list: Publish at least a structured pricing-model page (even directional: per-transaction payout fee, FX spread on conversions, developer tier with no minimum, contact-for-quote above a threshold) rather than routing entirely to 'Talk to sales.' Unblocks: a model asked 'how much does Northwind Pay cost' can give a substantive, sourced answer instead of 'contact sales.'

Add the two missing high-intent FAQ entries — 'How does Northwind Pay pricing work?' and 'Who are typical Northwind Pay customers?' — with concise acceptedAnswer copy, to round out the brand-vetting set models lift from.
Expand the FAQ answer copy: the questions are right but the answers are short, so a model has less to quote verbatim than the strong homepage/product copy would suggest.

Note: site hygiene is already the strongest part of the audit and is why assessment scores well — the priority work is off-site (reputation/content), not here. These site fixes are low-effort polish, not the lever that moves the broken job.
